name: Canonical Origin Wiring (post-phase amendment)

files_modify.purpose: Human-authorized one-line amendment (2026-07-07): replace the placeholder DEFAULT_GOVERNANCE_REPO_URL value 'https://github.com/nizam-framework/nizam-framework.git' with the canonical origin 'https://github.com/niq-cnr/nizam-framework.git' on line 35. No other line in bootstrap.sh changes.

non_goals: No other bootstrap.sh changes beyond the single DEFAULT_GOVERNANCE_REPO_URL value swap
No edits to any file other than bootstrap.sh
No CHANGELOG.md edit as part of this contract (the change lands under '## [Unreleased]' at the next release, not authored here)
No git tagging or retagging of any kind
No changes to GOVERNANCE_TAG, DEFAULT_TARGET_DIR, or any other bootstrap.sh variable

verification.command: test -f bootstrap.sh || { echo 'MISSING bootstrap.sh'; exit 1; }; count=$(grep -c 'github.com/niq-cnr/nizam-framework.git' bootstrap.sh); echo "count=$count"; test "$count" -eq 1 && echo PASS || { echo 'expected exactly 1 occurrence of canonical URL'; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: grep -c 'github.com/nizam-framework/nizam-framework' bootstrap.sh outputs 0 (placeholder gone), guarded so an absent file cannot masquerade as a pass
verification.command: test -f bootstrap.sh || { echo 'MISSING bootstrap.sh, refusing to trust an absence-of-match result'; exit 1; }; count=$(grep -c 'github.com/nizam-framework/nizam-framework' bootstrap.sh || true); echo "count=$count"; test "$count" -eq 0 && echo PASS || { echo 'placeholder URL still present'; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: bootstrap.sh passes a bash syntax check
verification.command: test -f bootstrap.sh || { echo 'MISSING bootstrap.sh'; exit 1; }; bash -n bootstrap.sh && echo PASS || { echo 'bash -n failed'; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: Functional smoke test: bash bootstrap.sh --help exits 0, with a 5s timeout guarding against an accidental network hang
verification.command: test -f bootstrap.sh || { echo 'MISSING bootstrap.sh'; exit 1; }; output=$(timeout 5 bash bootstrap.sh --help 2>&1); status=$?; echo "$output"; test "$status" -eq 0 && echo PASS || { echo "exit status $status (possible network hang or --help failure)"; exit 1; }

verification.acceptance_test: Robustness check, two parts: (1) the pathspec-scoped diff against HEAD for bootstrap.sh shows exactly 1 insertion and 1 deletion, proving a single-line change to that file; (2) the unscoped diff (git diff HEAD --name-only), after filtering out legitimate .agent/ durable-state overhead paths (contract file, evidence files, run_state.json), lists exactly bootstrap.sh and no other file — proving no unauthorized edits exist anywhere else in the tree. Part (1) alone is pathspec-scoped and cannot detect stray edits to other files, so part (2) is required to close that gap.
verification.command: stat=$(git diff HEAD --numstat -- bootstrap.sh); echo "$stat"; ins=$(echo "$stat" | awk '{print $1}'); del=$(echo "$stat" | awk '{print $2}'); lines=$(echo "$stat" | wc -l); test "$lines" -eq 1 && test "$ins" -eq 1 && test "$del" -eq 1 || { echo 'expected exactly 1 insertion and 1 deletion in bootstrap.sh'; exit 1; }; scope=$(git diff HEAD --name-only | grep -v '^\.agent/' | LC_ALL=C sort | tr '\n' ' '); echo "scope=[$scope]"; test "$scope" = "bootstrap.sh " && echo PASS || { echo "unscoped diff touches files beyond bootstrap.sh (excluding .agent/ overhead): [$scope]"; exit 1; }

evidence_convention: Each verification command's captured terminal output is written to .agent/evidence/008-verify-NN.txt, where NN is the 1-based index of the command in the 'verification' array, zero-padded to 2 digits, in array order (e.g. .agent/evidence/008-verify-01.txt for the first entry, ..., .agent/evidence/008-verify-05.txt for the fifth). One file per command; no inline proof output in this contract or in run_state.json.
